hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/TM5, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric discrimination against SSTR3/5, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with moderate selectivity gains, but off-target interactions remain a limiting factor. A more focused approach on non-pharmacophore positions with mutations that disrupt conserved pocket contacts is needed to push Δmargin further above 0.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions will allow targeted disruption of conserved pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations introduce charged or polar residues at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. These residues are unlikely to interfere with SSTR2 binding but can reduce off-target affinity.
